name: Astroknights
teaser: Play with a cast of ten unique minions that focus on drawing and playing actions that directly increase minion power.
mechanics: Play with a cast of ten unique minions that focus on drawing and playing actions that directly increase minion power.
description:
Meet the Astroknights, a faction from the Cease and Desist expansion. This group excels at discovering and utilizing actions that amplify the strength of their diverse minions. With a fortunate draw, their power can be unstoppable.

The Astroknights have a complexity rating of Low-Medium, making them accessible to a wide range of players. They are joined in the Cease and Desist set by three other factions: the Changerbots, Ignobles, and Star Roamers.
cardsTeaser:
The Astroknights faction is comprised of 10 unique minions and 10 actions, offering a balanced mix of gameplay possibilities. Their minions boast a total base power of 27, averaging 2.7 power per minion. Notably, several cards within the faction have the ability to augment minion power beyond these initial values.

Their action cards include a range of effects, such as two play-on-minion actions, Laser Sword, which appears twice. There's also one play-on-base action, Hidden Base. The majority of their actions are standard, with seven in total, including four that influence one or more minions: Block the Probe, It’s a Trap!, Prepare for Battle, Recycle the Trash, Use the Fours, and Yield to Rage, with Block the Probe and others providing direct power boosts. Specifically, six actions are designed to increase a minion's power: two instances of Block the Probe, two of Laser Sword, Use the Fours, and Yield to Rage.
actionList:
2x Block the Probe - Choose a minion. It gains +2 power until the end of the turn. Special: You may play this card before a base scores.
1x Hidden Base - Play on a base. Ongoing: At the start of your turn, if you have a minion here, draw a card.
1x It’s a Trap! - Special: Before a base scores, reveal cards from your deck until you reveal an action. You may draw it or play it immediately. Shuffle the unused cards into your deck.
2x Laser Sword - Play on a minion. Ongoing: This minion has +2 power and is not affected by other players’ minions.
1x Prepare for Battle - Reveal the top three cards of your deck. Draw two of them and return the other to the top or bottom of your deck.
1x Recycle the Trash - Shuffle up to two actions from your discard pile into your deck.
1x Use the Fours - One of your minions gains +4 power until the end of the turn.
1x Yield to Rage - One of your minions gains +2 power until the end of the turn. Play an extra action.
actions:
2x Block the Probe - Choose a minion. It gains +2 power until the end of the turn. Special: You may play this card before a base scores.

1x Hidden Base - Play on a base. Ongoing: At the start of your turn, if you have a minion here, draw a card.

1x It’s a Trap! - Special: Before a base scores, reveal cards from your deck until you reveal an action. You may draw it or play it immediately. Shuffle the unused cards into your deck.

2x Laser Sword - Play on a minion. Ongoing: This minion has +2 power and is not affected by other players’ minions.

1x Prepare for Battle - Reveal the top three cards of your deck. Draw two of them and return the other to the top or bottom of your deck.

1x Recycle the Trash - Shuffle up to two actions from your discard pile into your deck.

1x Use the Fours - One of your minions gains +4 power until the end of the turn.

1x Yield to Rage - One of your minions gains +2 power until the end of the turn. Play an extra action.
characters:
1x Space Knight - power 5 - Talent: Reveal the top three cards of your deck. You may draw one action from the revealed cards. Return the rest in any order.

1x Scoundrel - power 4 - Talent: Move this minion and another one of your minions here to another base.

1x Space Prince - power 4 - Talent: Play an extra action that directly increases the power of one of your minions.

1x Walking Carpet - power 4 - Special: Before this base scores, if you are not winning, you may play an action that directly increases the power of this minion.

1x Alien Guru - power 2 - Ongoing: Once per turn, after you play an action that directly increases a minion’s power, you may also place a +1 power counter on it.

1x Annoying Alien - power 2 - Talent: Name a minion. All minions here with that name get -2 power until the start of your next turn.

1x Astro Robot - power 2 - You may destroy an action played on this base or on a minion here.

1x Mannersbot - power 2 - Talent: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it is an action, draw it, otherwise return it.

1x Pupoks - power 2 - Talent: Lose 1 VP to reveal the top three cards of your deck and draw them. You may play one of them as an extra card.

1x Ghost Knight - power 0 - Ongoing: This minion cannot be destroyed. Your other minions here have +2 power.
bases:
* Hive of Scum and Villainy
* No-Moon™
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Cease and Desist rulebook:

Alien Guru: If the action increases the power of more than one minion, choose one of them to get the power counter.

Scoundrel: You can use its talent if you have no other minions at its base.

Space Prince, Walking Carpet, Alien Guru: A card directly increases power if it gives it “+N power”, or places a +1 power counter, or transfers a +1 power counter, or sets the printed power to a higher value. Granting a power-increase talent (e.g. The Touch), or destroying a negative power card (e.g. Change Into A Gun) are not direct power increases. This refers to actions that target cards already in play. (updated by The Bigger Geekier Box)
suggestionTeaser: Pairing Astroknights with factions that boost minion power through actions can create a formidable team.
synergy: When pairing with Astroknights, look for factions that pack a punch with actions that boost minion power. Some factions have only a handful of these powerful actions, while others are overflowing with them. Fortunately, this wiki provides a handy guide to identifying factions that possess these potent power-boosting abilities.
tips:
To maximize the Astroknights' potential, focus on timing your action plays strategically, as they typically boost minion power directly. Since many of these actions have a limited duration, often expiring at the end of the turn, it's crucial to reserve them for key moments. In less opportune turns, a more effective approach might be to simply play a minion or pass.

The Astroknights possess a significant capacity for card draw, but this advantage is contingent upon maintaining minions in play. If opponents dismantle your forces or you need to destroy a base, this draw potential may be temporarily inaccessible. Additionally, abilities like Pupoks' rely on having victory points to sacrifice, rendering them ineffective if you have no points to lose.
effects:
The Astroknights faction distills the essence of a galaxy far, far away into a unique set of mechanics, streamlining complexity while retaining the spirit of adventure. This faction focuses on a core set of abilities, applied in diverse ways to create a cohesive playstyle. At its heart, the Astroknights rely on a few key elements: every minion is one-of-a-kind, and the faction prioritizes simplicity and bold action.

Upon closer inspection, the Astroknights' array of abilities becomes apparent, featuring enhancements to movement, power reduction, defensive protection, and the ability to dismantle opponents' actions. While the faction may initially seem intricate, its various components work together to form a unified whole, making it more accessible than it initially appears.

name: Changerbots
teaser: Play play-on-minion actions, use talents to switch your minions between a powerful form and a weaker form with a useful ability, and move your minions around.
mechanics: Play play-on-minion actions, use talents to switch your minions between a powerful form and a weaker form with a useful ability, and move your minions around.
description: Meet the Changerbots, a faction from the Cease and Desist expansion that excels at adapting to any situation. They utilize talents that let you toggle between powerful minions and utility-focused ones, giving you flexibility in your gameplay. With a low complexity rating, the Changerbots are easy to pick up and play. They are joined in the Cease and Desist set by the Astroknights, Ignobles, and Star Roamers, offering a range of strategies to explore.
cardsTeaser:
The Changerbots faction boasts a standard complement of 10 minions and 10 actions. Notably, their minions pack a significant punch, with a total base power of 32, averaging 3.2 power per minion. This faction's cards also feature a range of abilities that can dynamically adjust minion power, adding a layer of strategy to gameplay. As an added bonus, the Changerbots have been fortunate enough to receive a titan from the TITANS Event Kit.

Their action cards are comprised of several key types, including eight play-on-minion actions: Cesium Armor, two instances of Change Into A Gun, Flighterizer, Matrix of Bossiness, Passengers, and two instances of The Touch. The faction also features two standard actions, Change Up and Roll On and Form Mergacon, which influence one or more minions. Furthermore, three actions - Cesium Armor, Form Mergacon, and Matrix of Bossiness - are specifically designed to boost a minion's power, offering opportunities for tactical maneuvering.
actionList:
1x Cesium Armor - Play on a minion. Ongoing: This minion has +2 power and cannot be destroyed.
2x Change Into A Gun - Play on one of your minions to destroy a minion of power 4 or less there. Ongoing: This minion has -2 power. Destroy this card at the start of your turn.
1x Change Up and Roll On - Special: Before a base scores you may move any number of your minions to there. They each get -1 power until the end of the turn.
1x Flighterizer - Play on one of your minions. Ongoing: This minion has the ability: “Talent: Move this minion to another base and give it -1 power until the end of the turn.”
1x Form Mergacon - Choose a base. Each of your minions there gains +1 power until the end of the turn.
1x Matrix of Bossiness - Play on one of your minions. Ongoing: This minion’s printed power is treated as if it were 5.
1x Passengers - Play on a minion. Ongoing: This minion has the ability: “Talent: If this minion moved this turn, move another one of your minions from the previous base to here.”
2x The Touch - Play on one of your minions. Ongoing: This minion has the ability: “Talent: This minion gains +3 power and loses all other abilities until the end of the turn.”
actions:
1x Cesium Armor - Play on a minion. Ongoing: This minion has +2 power and cannot be destroyed.

2x Change Into A Gun - Play on one of your minions to destroy a minion of power 4 or less there. Ongoing: This minion has -2 power. Destroy this card at the start of your turn.

1x Change Up and Roll On - Special: Before a base scores you may move any number of your minions to there. They each get -1 power until the end of the turn.

1x Flighterizer - Play on one of your minions. Ongoing: This minion has the ability: “Talent: Move this minion to another base and give it -1 power until the end of the turn.”

1x Form Mergacon - Choose a base. Each of your minions there gains +1 power until the end of the turn.

1x Matrix of Bossiness - Play on one of your minions. Ongoing: This minion’s printed power is treated as if it were 5.

1x Passengers - Play on a minion. Ongoing: This minion has the ability: “Talent: If this minion moved this turn, move another one of your minions from the previous base to here.”

2x The Touch - Play on one of your minions. Ongoing: This minion has the ability: “Talent: This minion gains +3 power and loses all other abilities until the end of the turn.”
characters:
1x Leader Two - power 6 - Talent: One of your other minions gains +2 power until the end of the turn.

2x Solarshout - power 4 - Talent: Play an extra action. Give this minion -2 power until the end of the turn.

3x Bruiser - power 2 - Ongoing: This minion cannot be destroyed. Talent: This minion gains +2 power and loses its Ongoing ability and all other abilities until the start of your next turn.

4x Huffie - power 3 - Talent: Move this minion to another base and give it -1 power until the end of the turn.
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Cease and Desist rulebook:

Bruiser, The Touch: When the talent is used, the minion loses the abilities granted by actions (e.g., The Touch, Passengers, Flighterizer), but not the benefits of an action’s own abilities (e.g., Cesium Armor, Matrix of Bossiness). Abilities gained after the talent is used are not lost. (updated by The Bigger Geekier Box)

Solarshout: Once triggered, the -2 power lasts even if it loses its ability (e.g. from The Touch).

Form Mergacon: This does not affect minions played after this card. (added by The Bigger Geekier Box)
effects: The Changerbots faction excels at manipulating their minions' abilities, allowing them to transform and adapt to changing circumstances. By switching between different statuses, Changerbot minions can gain a significant power boost or reduce their power in exchange for additional capabilities, such as enhanced movement or indestructibility. Furthermore, these minions can be augmented with extra talents, increasing their versatility and potential impact on the game. However, activating these talents often comes with trade-offs, like decreased power or lost abilities. As a result, players must carefully consider the order in which they activate their minions' talents, as some abilities may restrict the use of others, limiting a minion's overall effectiveness on a given turn.

name: Ignobles
teaser: Give control of your minions to other players for benefits, and take back control of them when the time is right.
mechanics: Give control of your minions to other players for benefits, and take back control of them when the time is right.
description: Meet the Ignobles, a faction from the Cease and Desist expansion that thrives on manipulation. They specialize in a unique strategy, willingly surrendering control of their minions to other players, only to reclaim them later. This unorthodox approach earns them a complexity rating of High. The Ignobles are joined in this set by three other factions: the Astroknights, Changerbots, and Star Roamers.
cardsTeaser:
The Ignobles faction is comprised of 10 minions and 10 actions, boasting a total base power of 30 among their minions, which averages out to 3 power per minion. Notably, they have also been granted a titan from the TITANS Event Kit, a rare distinction among factions.

Their action cards are varied, with all 10 being standard actions that influence one or more minions in different ways. Specific actions include Activate the Spy, which appears twice, as well as Banner Call, Fate of the Favorites, Hostage Exchange, Inevitable Betrayal, Out of Sight, Red Birthday Party, and Repaying Debts, also appearing twice. There are no actions that directly enhance a minion's power or are specifically designed to be played on minions or bases.
actionList:
2x Activate the Spy - Take control of a minion you own. Play an extra action.
1x Banner Call - Take control of any number of minions you own.
1x Fate of the Favorites - Each player destroys a minion they own.
1x Hostage Exchange - Give another player control of one of your minions to take control of one of their minions of equal or less power.
1x Inevitable Betrayal - Special: Before a base scores, take control of a minion you own there.
1x Out of Sight - Each player returns a minion they own to their hand.
1x Red Birthday Party - Destroy a minion you own and all minions with less power on the same base.
2x Repaying Debts - Give control of one of your minions to another player to draw two cards and play an extra minion.
actions:
2x Activate the Spy - Take control of a minion you own. Play an extra action.

1x Banner Call - Take control of any number of minions you own.

1x Fate of the Favorites - Each player destroys a minion they own.

1x Hostage Exchange - Give another player control of one of your minions to take control of one of their minions of equal or less power.

1x Inevitable Betrayal - Special: Before a base scores, take control of a minion you own there.

1x Out of Sight - Each player returns a minion they own to their hand.

1x Red Birthday Party - Destroy a minion you own and all minions with less power on the same base.

2x Repaying Debts - Give control of one of your minions to another player to draw two cards and play an extra minion.
characters:
1x Aunt of Drakes - power 5 - Talent: Give control of one of your minions to another player to draw a card and play an extra action.

2x Foot of the King - power 4 - Ongoing: At the end of your turn, you may take control of a minion you own.

3x Betrothed - power 3 - You may give control of this minion to another player. Ongoing: The owner of this minion gains 1 VP if another player wins this base and controls this minion.

4x Sneaky Squire - power 2 - You may give control of this minion to another player to play an extra minion.
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Cease and Desist rulebook:

Activate the Spy, Banner Call, Foot of the King, Inevitable Betrayal: These target minions from your deck whether or not you still control them.

Betrothed: The ability works regardless of how control was gained, and even if the Betrothed is moved to another base.
synergy:
When combining Ignobles with other factions, several synergies emerge. Pairing with Kitty Cats allows you to reclaim your Sneaky Squires and potentially destroy them to trigger effects like Whiskers, Cat Fight, or Nine Lives. The tandem of Aunt of Drakes and Queen Fluffy enables the theft of weak minions from other players, which can be traded for card draw or returned to your control after being given away.

Teaming up with Sharks enables the destruction of Sneaky Squires to play Makos or bolster Hammerheads and other minions using Chum. Russian Fairy Tales offers opportunities to play additional minions by leveraging Sneaky Squires and Collecting Debts, and then transforming minions you own but other players control into extra minions under your command.

Mermaids complement Ignobles nicely, as a Captive Audience can be built using Sneaky Squires and Betrothed. Toll Bay can increase card draw, while Charmers and Charmed can easily move minions with a power of 3 or less, empowering Temptresses in the process. However, Sirens can diminish the contribution of these minions, and Becalmed Shores can nullify their power entirely, which may not be ideal for Betrothed. Siren Song can be utilized to gather shared minions at one base, setting up a Banner Call or following up on Activate the Spy.
effects:
The Ignobles' core strategy revolves around temporarily relinquishing control of your minions to opponents in exchange for beneficial effects, such as drawing cards or gaining extra plays. However, this approach requires careful consideration, as surrendering control of your minions effectively increases your opponent's power. To offset this loss, the Ignobles rely on the advantages gained from extra card plays, particularly minion plays, to compensate for the temporary power shift. Additionally, drawing cards enables you to access the resources needed to reclaim control of your minions and accelerate your gameplay.

If you're unable to regain control of the minions you've lent, the Ignobles can also employ alternative tactics, such as destroying or returning them to reduce your opponent's overall power and mitigate the consequences of their temporary advantage. By navigating this delicate balance of power and strategy, the Ignobles can turn a potentially disadvantageous situation into a winning opportunity.

name: Star Roamers
teaser: Return your minions to hand for benefits, and manipulate how your minions are affected, like moving them instead of returning them or having your power-2 minions be affected instead of others.
mechanics: Return your minions to hand for benefits, and manipulate how your minions are affected, like moving them instead of returning them or having your power-2 minions be affected instead of others.
description: Meet the Star Roamers, a faction from the Cease and Desist expansion, bringing a unique playstyle to the table. By leveraging the ability to return minions to their owner's hand, the Star Roamers achieve a high degree of mobility and protection. This faction is suited for players who enjoy strategic maneuvering, with a complexity rating of Medium-High. The Star Roamers join the Astroknights, Changerbots, and Ignobles as part of the Cease and Desist set, offering an exciting new dimension to the game.
cardsTeaser:
The Star Roamers faction is composed of 10 minions and 10 actions, offering a balanced mix of gameplay options. Their minions boast a total base power of 30, averaging 3 power per minion. 

In terms of actions, the Star Roamers have a range of options at their disposal. They feature three play-on-base actions: Protector Fields, Weird New Worlds, and Whiplash Maneuver. The remaining seven actions are standard, with all of them impacting one or more minions in various ways. These include Hyperspeed 10, two instances of Mass Teleport, two instances of Port Me Up, Teleport Error, and Teleport Overflow. Notably, none of the actions in this faction directly enhance a minion's power, and there are no play-on-minion actions to be found.
actionList:
1x Hyperspeed 10 - Choose a base. Move all minions from there to a single other base.
2x Mass Teleport - Each player returns one of their minions to its owner’s hand.
2x Port Me Up - Return one of your minions to its owner’s hand. Special: You may play this after a base scores; the minion must be on the scoring base.
1x Protector Fields - Play on a base where you have a minion. Ongoing: Your minions here are not affected by other players’ cards.
1x Teleport Error - Choose a minion. Move it to another random base.
1x Teleport Overflow - Return one of your minions to its owner’s hand. Reveal any number of minions with the same name from your hand, and play them as extra minions.
1x Weird New Worlds - Play a new base. You may play an extra minion there.
1x Whiplash Maneuver - Play on a base. Ongoing: If any of your minions on other bases would be returned to your hand, you may move them here instead.
actions:
1x Hyperspeed 10 - Choose a base. Move all minions from there to a single other base.

2x Mass Teleport - Each player returns one of their minions to its owner’s hand.

2x Port Me Up - Return one of your minions to its owner’s hand. Special: You may play this after a base scores; the minion must be on the scoring base.

1x Protector Fields - Play on a base where you have a minion. Ongoing: Your minions here are not affected by other players’ cards.

1x Teleport Error - Choose a minion. Move it to another random base.

1x Teleport Overflow - Return one of your minions to its owner’s hand. Reveal any number of minions with the same name from your hand, and play them as extra minions.

1x Weird New Worlds - Play a new base. You may play an extra minion there.

1x Whiplash Maneuver - Play on a base. Ongoing: If any of your minions on other bases would be returned to your hand, you may move them here instead.
characters:
1x Ship’s Captain - power 5 - Search your deck for a minion, reveal it, add it to your hand and shuffle your deck. If it has power 3 or less, play it here as an extra minion.

1x Medical Officer - power 4 - Ongoing: When one or more of your other minions would be returned to your hand, you may draw a card instead of returning one of them.

1x Science Officer - power 4 - Talent: Return one of your minions of power 4 or less to its owner’s hand. Play an extra minion with a different name.

3x Ship’s Engineer - power 3 - Ongoing: If another one of your minions would be returned to your hand, you may move it to another base instead.

4x Ensign - power 2 - Ongoing: When an opponent plays a card that directly affects one of your other minions here, you may have it affect this minion instead.
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Cease and Desist rulebook:

Ensign: It can take on ongoing or special effects, but not from cards already in play. It can take on multiple effects if it makes sense (e.g. multiple -1 power, but not multiple destroys unless it’s immune). It can have an action played on a different minion played on itself instead. It can substitute for a different minion being taken control of. It cannot take an effect already taken by a different Ensign from a third minion. It can take an effect even if it does not fit the original target’s prerequisites (e.g. “power 3 or higher”).

Science Officer, Teleport Overflow: You can return a minion you control but don’t own, and still do the rest of the ability.

Ship’s Engineer, Whiplash Maneuver: These don’t trigger if you return a minion you don’t own, since it doesn’t go to your hand.

Teleport Overflow: The minions don’t have to have the same name as the returned minion, but have the same name as each other. A single minion counts as a group with the same name.

Weird New Worlds: If this card leaves play before the next base scores, the number of bases is increased for the rest of the game.
suggestionTeaser: The Star Roamers shine brightest when paired with factions that specialize in pulling minions back to their owner's hand, often combining this ability with impressive on-play effects.
synergy:
To unlock the full potential of the Star Roamers, pair them with factions that excel at returning minions to their owner's hand, often accompanied by powerful on-play effects. The Medical Officer is particularly useful in mitigating the drawbacks of self-destruction, although she doesn't provide benefits for abilities that rely on minion destruction.

Combining Star Roamers with Aliens proves to be a potent mix, as the Invader's exceptional on-play ability complements the Star Roamers' capabilities. Aliens also bring additional on-play effects to the table, which work seamlessly with Ship's Captain, and their ability to send enemy minions back to their owner's hand can be a game-changer. Furthermore, the Scout makes an ideal target for Teleport Overflow.

Shapeshifters also make a great match for Star Roamers, as they can more easily find and utilize Ship's Captain and the Officers. Moreover, the Medical Officer helps alleviate the destruction penalties associated with Shapeshifter actions, creating a more stable foundation for the Star Roamers.

Ninjas offer another intriguing combination, as their Acolyte's ability can be used to effectively "return" the minion and generate an extra play every turn. The Ninjas' on-play abilities also help offset the Star Roamers' lack of destructive power, and Infiltrate can even be used to destroy Weird New Worlds.

Lastly, Fairies can be a promising partner for the Star Roamers, as the Science Officer can return powerful minions like Titania and Ship's Captain when their power is reduced. This can be achieved using Glymmer or Enchantment, allowing for rapid deployment of top-tier minions. Alternatively, Puck can be used to gain extra cards or actions, further enhancing the Star Roamers' capabilities.
tips:
Mastering the Star Roamers requires navigating a complex web of synergies and trade-offs. Since you'll rarely have all your minions on the table simultaneously, you must constantly adapt to the evolving game state. As opponents remove your key minions, you'll need to make tough decisions about which combinations to prioritize with your limited minion plays. Keeping track of your options can be overwhelming, especially amidst the chaos of a multiplayer game.

The Ship's Captain is a crucial asset in assembling a cohesive team. Don't underestimate the value of discovering your strongest minions, even if you can't play them immediately. You'll often face difficult choices between leveraging your minions' abilities or utilizing their power to break bases. Ideally, you want to balance both. Port Me Up is particularly useful in achieving this balance. By combining Big Drops and Bait and Switches, the Star Roamers can unleash coordinated attacks from multiple angles, but integrating these elements is a challenging and dynamic puzzle to solve.
effects:
The Star Roamers faction excels at manipulating minion movement, leveraging teleportation-like effects to control the return of minions to your hand. As more minions are in play, their combined abilities create a chain reaction, granting enhanced protection and mobility. However, their capabilities are largely focused on self-sustainment, with limited direct impact on opposing players.

This chain reaction can be triggered through various means, allowing minions to return to your hand or move to any base. Certain cards, such as Science Officer and standard actions, initiate this return, while Medical Officer intervenes when another minion is about to be destroyed. Alternatively, Ship's Engineer and Whiplash Maneuver enable you to redirect returning minions to a different base, conserving valuable plays and unlocking additional benefits from Science Officer.

Ensigns serve as guardians for your high-value Officers and Engineers, diverting harmful effects away from them. In return, Officers and Engineers, particularly Medical Officer, can sacrifice Ensigns to send them back into action. The Ship's Captain stands out as the only Star Roamer minion with an on-play ability, making it an ideal choice for returning to your hand instead of relocating to another base.

The Star Roamers deck also features unique cards like Weird New Worlds, which can increase the number of bases in play beyond the standard limit. Teleport Overflow is another notable action, allowing you to play multiple minions with the same name on any base, including the one you just returned to your hand. This versatility makes it a valuable addition to the faction's arsenal.
